review_text: Seen this style of film before but definitely one of the best, Love the ending.
This movie messed up my brain in the most perfect way it could

One of the most unique cinematic viewing experiences I've ever had. The film's unorthodox narrative structure jumps around time periods in the main character's life. The black and white scenes are shown chronologically, while the color scenes are shown in reverse. It's an amazing premise of a detective who can't make new memories having to find creative ways with photographs to maintain short-term memories he cannot sustain in order to gather clues and evidence about the disappearance of his wife. It immediately hooks you into the story and makes you wonder how he's going to solve this case in a very unpredictable movie. The acting is terrific. The movie has great cinematography. The movie really makes you wonder whether it's Teddy or Natalie who's manipulating Leonard's memory. Your opinions of the characters are constantly changing and it's constantly keeping you guessing. It's a fantastic movie with a very creative ways at presenting the story and unfolding the mystery.
This is the most unique and creative movie I've ever seen! This is the reason why I love movies so much. Christopher Nolan is a genius!
I’ve seen this movie three times and still catch something I missed the previous viewing. Unique plot, unpredictable twists, just awesome.
Loved from the first time I watched 20-years ago - cult classic!

What an absolutely fantastic movie, brilliantly acted and the plot is great and the back to front scenes work brilliantly to keep you guessing as to what actually has gone on. Id never heard of it before and only came across it by chance on a streaming app and checked Rotten Tomatoes to see the reviews which is why I gave it a try and very happy that I did. Just great all the way through and no let downs, a must watch if you like a good thriller.

What an amazingly original movie! I have never seen anything like it! I loved the format of the movie going from the present all the way into the past by scene. The sense of anticipation and wondering what the truth really is. But what really is the truth when the main protagonist doesn't know what's the truth? The dialogue and acting was also amazing. This is a landmark movie.
definitely one of my fav. movie. amazing cast and great suspense i would definitely recommend it and absolute cinema

This is the best movie I have ever watched in my entire life and I have seen a whole lot of movies. I was searching for a psychological mind bender and that is exactly what I found. Everything about this movie is made extremely well. It's interesting keeps you thinking and isn't one of those extremely predictable movies. I'm definitely going to watch this again even though it was something I came across when I was desperate for a movie. This is genuinely my new favorite movie. I fully recommend these masterpiece of cinematography.
Phenomenal. Enough said.
Beautiful movie. A hands down 10/10 movie, such good storytelling and the reveal was just executed perfectly 👌
My favorite Nolan film. We need a part 2.
Very disjointed and confusing. Strange enigmatic conclusion
Not necessarily the easiest to follow (obviously), but an interesting story, and an enjoyable watch

Take a picture, write it down, tattoo it to your body, "Go see the movie Memento". It has been a while since I have been moved into mental delirium and intellectual fatigue by a motion picture as I was with Memento. Leonard Shelby, played by chillingly by Guy Pearce (LA Confidential), is a man who suffers from a mental condition that does not allow him to make new memories. He has no short-term memory. The last thing he remembers is seeing his wife raped and murdered. Now armed with a Polaroid camera and a pen he searches for revenge. Directed by Christopher Nolan, who also co-wrote the screenplay with this brother, whose short story inspired the movie, doesn't just reply on the great plot to tell the story. He tells it backwards. Thus the beauty of Memento. We leave the theater feeling the same type of dazed confusion that Shelby must feel every 10 minutes. This is a film that begs for your intelligence, attention and ironically, and yes, your memory.

Great non linear story. Gets a bit repetitive watching the same scenes repeat over and over, but Christopher Nolan is no stranger to poking his viewers brains repeatedly, making you scratch your head till it hurts. The acting is great and the mood is very sinister and psychotic. Doesn't have a great climax like other movies but that's the idea since the movie is completely non linear. Overall if you've watched other Nolan movies and liked them or need a starting point for his psych thrillers this is a great place to start... Or end?

UUUUUHHHHH, I absolutely hate Christopher Nolan movies, but I'll rate this one I guess... Did I like it? Hell no. Confusing as f**k, and if you know me, I HATE confusing movies. (I guess I wouldn't do very well with Lost Highway then)... Anyway, I don't really feel like watching it again. I know it's really famous though.
A true feat of filmmaking process. How in the world did they storyboard this?

What can I say? Christopher Nolan always cooks. Memento is another perfect example of how he plays with time like no one else. The story is told backward through the eyes of Leonard, a man with short-term memory loss, and it fits so well with his condition that it almost feels natural. Every scene pulls you in, making you question what’s real and what’s not until that twist hits and completely flips what you thought you knew. What I really love is how it’s not just about a clever structure. It’s about how people lie to themselves just to keep going, and that hits hard. Guy Pearce absolutely nails it, and the whole film still feels sharp and fresh even after all these years. I’d definitely recommend it one of Nolan’s most creative works.

"This is the first time I have watched a movie where the plot is played backwards. It captivizes you, making you want to know who did it and how. The plot twist at the end is something I had already figured out once I put the pieces together."

Uma obra-prima que sempre vale a pena ser revista. Memento é genial em diversos níveis, mas principalmente na forma como Nolan conduz o expectador em um emaranhado de situações que se revelam cada vez mais confusas, revelando a condição dramática do protagonista a lá mito de Sísifo.

5.0 stars; The most unique, creative film I have ever seen. A masterpiece in filmmaking. The film's protagonist has short-term memory issues and is trying to find the man who raped and killed his wife. The unique concept for this movie is that his story is told in reverse. You are so captivated by this imaginative plot scheme that it's easy to miss superb performances by Guy Pearce and Carrie-Anne Moss. This film garnered Academy Award nominations and numerous independent film society awards. It is one of my favorite films of all time.
One of my favourite movies.
This movie is my favourite movie
Nolans best movie by far, amazing script and Guy Pearce is fantastic!
confusing but good overall with great acting

Wow, I was locked into this movie from the start. The way how Christopher Nolan starts from the end at the beginning of the movie and we go back to the beginning at the end to find out what happened was brilliant.
A complete masterpiece throughout the ages
Super dope hella cool not boring def should watch
Really gets you thinking, classic Christopher Nolan masterpiece! You need to lock in for this kind of film and can’t miss a second but crazy good!

This movie made me feel as powerless and vulnerable as Leonard, which in turn made me relate to his character more in response to the betrayal he experienced without his knowing. I felt equally as out of control and confused as the protagonist because of the backwards way the movie is filmed which worked so well with the main theme the movie left me with; everyone will take you for granted, take advantage of you, and use you for there personal gain if you let them. The tragedy here is that Leonard never got the chance to let them, rather he was doomed from the start, and will never know it ever even happened. Pure cinema, 10/10.
Perfection. Unparalleled originality. Far and away Nolan’s best feat, and laid the groundwork for subsequent films.
Every now and then a movie gets made where you feel like it is the first to market with a concept and near perfect in creation. This is one of them.

Absolutely harrowing commentary on the human experiences of memory, love, and most importantly denial. Very much a movie you can think about for hours after watching, and my personal favorite from Christopher Nolan. Non-linear storytelling is very hard to pull off correctly, and this movie goes above and beyond correct, it just may be perfect.
Complicated, but intriguing at the same time.
Quite possible one of the greatest films of all time. It takes risks, has a great cast, and gets better every time.e you watch it.
One of the brain rotting movies, lol. At one point I thought have i watched this movie before
Nolan's second feature is, in many ways, a masterpiece of clever storytelling.

Very underrated movie in Christopher Nolan library films he did a wonderful job on this film. What I like about this film was the execution of it how he shot it and how he told the story in a manner a way that show his artistic touch and his creative directing skills. Overall, among movie fanatics this is must watch, I waited a while to see this movie but I am glad I got to.

Nolan definitely achieved what he was aiming to do when he made this film. Thought it was really creative to tell the story how he did. A pretty simple plot that turns way more complicated. I really enjoy how we are basically put into this story, and we are following along with the main character as to what the issue is. We basically are the main character, just in a first person POV. Now the fact that I have a million questions after this is prolly a good thing, but it really doesn't after my learning of the main story. It is simple questions that make me feel like a PI. I'll have to watch the film a few more times now that I have a grasp of things. Overall, pretty blown away with what Nolan chose to tell us. For something that could have easily failed, I'd say he pulled it off quite nice.
Very interesting and clever movie. Some might see it as a little odd but definitely very creative.
Christopher Nolans first great film. The editing and story are awesome. It doesn't need loads of action to keep you entertained. Just a great story.
Great original premise makes up Memento for being a classic
A total headache of a movie (im pretty sure that was Nolan intention), that is hard to follow and not for me.
Smart. Intelligent. Genius. Christopher Nolan.
Didn’t like it when it came out years ago. Saw it again recently and disliked it less. But not by much. What’s that all about, eh?
One trippy movie. As convoluted as it was with the timeline and sequence of events, it wasn't too difficult to follow (unlike Tenet).
